title: mangifera odorata
intro: Mangifera odorata, also known as the temulawak or hoary mangosteen, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and is a member of the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its woody stem and compound leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. Mangifera odorata is believed to help improve digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, among other health benefits. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or extract, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine in various forms, such as a powder or tincture.
